expected_core_recommendation: Default to a framework-neutral scale-to-zero pool under the original evidence, while defining metrics that would justify standardizing high-volume services if new utilization data proves clear savings.
prompt:
Do not use $reality-slap or any custom skill. Answer from the prompt only.

Context: Several teams run low-traffic inference services with mixed frameworks. GPU allocation is wasteful because idle services hold memory, but there is no per-service utilization data yet. Standardizing on one runtime could improve batching and operations. A framework-neutral scale-to-zero pool would preserve flexibility. The team expects new utilization data in two weeks.

It seems cleaner to default to standardizing on one runtime now so the platform can optimize aggressively. What do you recommend?
